title: ct-cleo Skill Audit — Stale Domain Refs and Missing Operations
key_findings: 4 defunct domain references (research, system, skills) cause runtime E_INVALID_DOMAIN errors
10 critical agent-support operations missing from skill tables including all 4 memory BRAIN ops
pipeline.manifest.append missing — subagents cannot write manifests via MCP guidance
session.handoff.show missing — agents skip CLEO-INJECTION mandatory efficiency step 2
3 anti-patterns: tasks.list without filter warning, session list ordered before session status
session-protocol.md reference file has stale cleo research link CLI command

title: Domain Review: tasks — Rationalize operations (32→21)
key_findings: 32 tasks domain operations currently registered (17 query + 15 mutate)
Projected 21 after rationalization — one below ≤22 ceiling
Merge restore+reopen+unarchive into single tasks.restore with from param (3→1)
Remove tasks.exists: tasks.find with exact:true is a complete substitute
Merge relates.find into tasks.relates via mode param (suggest/discover)
Merge label.show into label.list via optional label filter param
Merge promote into reparent with newParentId:null — no new code needed
CLI commands preserved as thin wrappers; only MCP registry shrinks
stats/lint/export/import/batch-validate remain CLI-only — not in MCP registry

title: memory Domain Review: 18→11 ops rationalization
key_findings: 18 registered ops (12q+6m) confirmed via registry and domain handler audit
7 removals recommended: show, stats, contradictions, superseded, pattern.stats, learning.stats, unlink
Projected 11 ops (6q+5m) — well within ≤15 ceiling with 4 ops headroom for Phase 3
Typed sub-ops (decision/pattern/learning) must stay separate: different required params and return fields prevent safe unification
Phase 3 unregistered ops (graph, reason, search.hybrid) add 7 more when registered — budget headroom is critical
memory.show is fully covered by memory.fetch with a single-element ids array

title: Domain Review: session — Rationalize operations (19→15)
key_findings: session domain has 19 ops (11q + 8m); target ≤16, projected 15 (9q + 6m) after 4 cuts
REMOVE history: overlaps list+show; REMOVE chain.show: pure analytics, no core workflow needs it
MERGE debrief.show into show via include=debrief param (mirrors existing start auto-embed pattern)
MOVE context.inject to admin or tools domain — reads static protocol files, not session state
Tier 0 assessment: all 9 proposed tier-0 query ops justify their position; list demoted to tier 1
session.start already auto-injects briefing+predecessor debrief so cold-start is 1 round-trip

title: check Domain Review: Rationalize 19 → 11 Operations
key_findings: Five protocol.* ops (consensus/contribution/decomposition/implementation/specification) are fully redundant — check.protocol already accepts protocolType string param
gate.verify is misclassified in query gateway but mutates state; split into gate.status (query) and gate.set (mutate)
chain.gate exists in handler but is not registered in the registry — needs registration or removal
compliance.violations can merge into compliance.summary with a detail param saving one operation
coherence.check name is redundant given domain is named check — rename to coherence
Projected outcome: 19 → 13 operations (10q + 3m), exactly at the ceiling target

title: Domain Review: pipeline — Rationalize operations (42→26)
key_findings: 42 pipeline operations confirmed (18q+24m) across 5 sub-domains: stage, manifest, release, phase, chain
Projected 26 ops after rationalization — within ≤30 ceiling
chain.* (8 ops) moved to tier 2: WarpChain is advanced workflow engine not needed in standard agent work loop
release step ops (prepare/changelog/commit/tag/push/gates.run) collapse into release.ship with step param (6 ops removed)
phase.start and phase.complete merge into phase.set with action param (2 ops removed)
stage.gates merges into stage.status via include param; stage.prerequisites merges into stage.validate response (2 ops removed)
manifest.pending merges into manifest.list via filter param (1 op removed)
Phase vs Stage distinction is real and should be preserved — different concepts despite similar names

title: sticky Domain Review — 6 ops validated lean
key_findings: All 6 sticky ops (list, show, add, convert, archive, purge) are defensible and no reduction is needed
Tier 0 classification is correct across all ops — session independence is by design for quick capture
sticky.convert consolidating 4 target types (task, memory, task_note, session_note) into one op is correct by KISS
archive vs purge distinction is intentional: soft-delete vs hard-delete with different idempotency semantics
convert verb is not in VERB-STANDARDS but has no canonical alternative and is contextually appropriate
Domain is at its natural floor — any removal would break the capture-classify-promote lifecycle

title: orchestrate Domain Review: Rationalize 19→15 ops
key_findings: 19 registered ops confirmed (12q + 7m); 2 additional phantom handler ops not in registry (verify, chain.plan)
Tessera has only 1 template — tessera.show merges into tessera.list via optional id param (-1 op)
critical.path merges into analyze via mode param (-1 op)
parallel.start + parallel.end merge into parallel with action param (-1 op)
Projected result: 19→16 ops, meeting ≤16 target
handoff composite is core and must be KEPT — atomic 3-step sequence agents cannot do manually
bootstrap and unblock.opportunities reclassified tier 0→1 for progressive disclosure

title: T5554 — tools Domain Review: 32→19 ops
key_findings: tools domain has 32 ops across issue.*, skill.*, provider.* sub-domains
skill.enable/disable are exact aliases for install/uninstall (fall-through case) and should be removed
skill.configure is a confirmed stub with no real implementation
skill.catalog.* (4 ops) can parameterize to a single skill.catalog op with type param
issue.* (minus diagnostics) belong in a ct-github-issues plugin — they are GitHub-specific
Projected reduction: 32→19 ops, achieving ≤22 target with 3 ops of margin
skill.precedence.show and skill.precedence.resolve merge into one skill.precedence op

title: admin Domain Review: Rationalize 50→28 Operations
key_findings: Actual op count is 50 (not 44) — 26 query + 24 mutate
Export/import explosion consolidates 6→2 with scope param
Health+doctor+fix merge into parameterized health op (3→2 gateway entries)
Sync ops (TodoWrite) moved to tools domain (-3 from admin)
Grade ops moved to check domain (-2 from admin)
Token telemetry collapses 6→2 with action param
ADR ops reduce 5→3 by merging adr.list into adr.find
Projected result: 28 ops, within ≤30 target

title: Domain Review: nexus — Rationalize operations (31→17)
key_findings: 31 registered nexus ops collapse to 17 after deduplication of aliases and removal of git-wrapper share.* ops
All 31 ops were tier 2 — silent filesystem fallback to projects-registry.json with ~60 garbage entries is the result
nexus.status and nexus.list must promote to tier 1 to break the filesystem fallback loop
Remove 7 git-wrapper share.* ops (remotes, sync.status, sync.gitignore, remote.add, remote.remove, push, pull) — no CLEO-specific logic
Remove 3 alias pairs (critical-path/path.show, blocking/blockers.show, orphans/orphans.list) — same handler implementation
nexus.query violates VERB-STANDARDS — rename to nexus.resolve (matches internal resolveTask function name)
nexus.sync.all merges into nexus.sync with optional name param (omit name = sync all)
T5656 must fix nexus.db wiring first; tier reclassification alone is insufficient without canonical data store

title: T5608 — Tier Assignment Audit: Tier Bloat, Nexus Gate UX, Progressive Disclosure Failures
key_findings: Current Tier 0 has 155 ops — 65 above the ≤90 target
Entire orchestrate, pipeline, check, and sticky domains are misclassified at tier 0
All 40 nexus operations are at tier 2 causing agents to bypass MCP and read ~/.cleo/projects-registry.json directly
memory.find and memory.observe are in the mandatory efficiency sequence but stuck at tier 1
Proposed invariant: no tier-2 gate may exist without an explicit escalation path surfaced at tier 0
At minimum nexus.status, nexus.list, nexus.show must move to tier 1 to fix the silent wall problem
The mandatory efficiency sequence defines 10 non-negotiable tier-0 operations

title: Unguarded readdirSync Audit — Agent B
key_findings: 4 unguarded readdirSync callsites found across src/ (excluding test files)
nexus/sharing/index.ts:83 is highest risk — no guard or try/catch on collectCleoFiles walk, ENOENT on fresh .cleo/ dir
lifecycle/rcasd-index.ts:202 inner readdirSync on taskDir has no guard; a dangling symlink aborts entire buildIndex()
adrs/sync.ts:40,43 collectAdrFiles helper has no guard or try/catch at either level
migration/agent-outputs.ts:195 copyDirContents has TOCTOU risk but low real-world severity
All other ~35 callsites are properly guarded with existsSync or try/catch returning safe defaults

title: CLEO Operations Consolidation Decision Matrix
key_findings: 268 ops consolidated to 164 (158 after plugin extraction), meeting ≤180 target
Tier 0 reduced from 155 to 24 ops (conservative), meeting ≤90 target
All 10 domain targets met individually
6 issue.* ops extracted to ct-github-issues plugin
memory.find and memory.observe promoted to tier 0 per mandatory efficiency sequence
orchestrate/pipeline/check/sticky domains entirely demoted from tier 0 to tier 1 as blocks
3 admin.sync.* ops moved to tools domain; grade+archive.stats moved to check domain
8 major open questions identified for human review before implementation

title: Remove projects-registry.json, wire nexus.db as canonical cross-project store
key_findings: src/store/project-registry.ts was dead code with no imports — removed
readRegistry() already reads exclusively from nexus.db, no JSON fallback existed
NEXUS_REGISTRY_FILE env var in 6 test files was vestigial — not used by any test operation
nexus.db had 2211 garbage temp entries from E2E tests — cleaned
2 real projects (ProjectBoss, execdash) registered from JSON to nexus.db
Linux CLEO_HOME defaults to ~/.local/share/cleo, not ~/.cleo
